jokes.text: Look at him, t-shirt, jeans. - Is it you? - I am flattered.
jokes.setup: Someone showing Jim a photo asking if he recognizes the person
jokes.punchline: Jim deadpans that he's flattered by the comparison
jokes.explanation: Classic Jim understated response - good character work and economy

jokes.text: That's johnny depp. I read in people magazine that he was looking for a two-bedroom condo in scranton.
jokes.setup: Andy believes he spotted Johnny Depp
jokes.punchline: The absurd specificity that Depp would want a condo in Scranton
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy delusion - celebrity obsession meets small-town reality

jokes.text: Where do I find the black pearl? Captain jack sparrow. John dillinger. No. I'm captain jack sparrow. Captain crunch.
jokes.setup: Andy trying to prove his Johnny Depp knowledge
jokes.punchline: Jim's mocking escalation ending with Captain Crunch
jokes.explanation: Good escalating absurdity with Jim's perfect deflating punchline

jokes.text: Remember how you felt when you thought you'd seen roger clemens? At the yankees game. - Yeah. Well, it wasn't him.
jokes.setup: Andy trying to get Jim to empathize with celebrity spotting excitement
jokes.punchline: Jim admitting his own false sighting
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing Jim's vulnerability

jokes.text: It also explains why the name on his mailbox was m. Shulman.
jokes.setup: Andy's Johnny Depp theory falling apart
jokes.punchline: Andy doubling down with mailbox 'evidence'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy logic - when reality contradicts delusion, incorporate reality into delusion

jokes.text: M. Night shulman?
jokes.setup: Andy's mention of M. Shulman
jokes.punchline: Jim connecting it to M. Night Shyamalan
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay - perfect timing and Jim's quick wit

jokes.text: The lost and found has gone missing. It itself is lost.
jokes.setup: Michael announcing office updates
jokes.punchline: The meta-joke about the lost and found being lost
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay with good structure - classic Michael absurdist observation

jokes.text: You couldn't handle my undivided attention.
jokes.setup: Michael asking for undivided attention from sales staff
jokes.punchline: Andy's cocky retort implying his attention is too powerful
jokes.explanation: Good Andy arrogance - the absurdity of attention being too intense

jokes.text: Make friends first, make sales second, make love third... in no particular order.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining the old office philosophy
jokes.punchline: The contradiction of 'in no particular order' after giving a specific order
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael Scott logic contradiction - highly quotable and character-specific

jokes.text: Including the car phone!
jokes.setup: Andy demanding Michael call all six of his numbers
jokes.punchline: The anachronistic mention of a car phone
jokes.explanation: Good character detail - Andy's outdated tech references showing his pretension

jokes.text: Salesman is king. As the best salesman, I am king of kings. Oh, you say jesus is king of kings? Well, what does that say to you about how I think of myself?
jokes.setup: Dwight explaining his superiority as a salesman
jokes.punchline: The blasphemous implication that he equals Jesus
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Dwight megalomania - perfect escalation to religious comparison with his characteristic obliviousness

jokes.text: Honey, if I don't have time to answer an email, I definitely don't have time to walk over to your desk.
jokes.setup: Pam asking why Jim doesn't answer emails
jokes.punchline: The logical fallacy that walking takes more time than typing
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about workplace logic - relatable but not exceptional

jokes.text: Wow wowie wowie.
jokes.setup: Jim seeing Andy's commission check
jokes.punchline: The childish, sarcastic expression of amazement
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective Jim sarcasm - the repetitive 'wowie' is perfectly dismissive

jokes.text: Stop sexting pam.
jokes.setup: Jim typing while Andy tries to talk
jokes.punchline: Andy's assumption about what Jim is typing
jokes.explanation: Solid character moment - Andy's projection and assumption

jokes.text: You writing your memoirs over there? You writing your name over there?
jokes.setup: Jim taking time to write something
jokes.punchline: The escalation from memoirs to just writing his name
jokes.explanation: Good escalating impatience from Andy - the childish final insult works

jokes.text: There's other pencils in this office. Gimme--I--
jokes.setup: Andy demanding Jim give him a pencil
jokes.punchline: Jim's reasonable response followed by Andy's continued demanding
jokes.explanation: Functional cringe comedy - Andy's unreasonable demands create awkwardness

jokes.text: Honey and jelly sandwich time.
jokes.setup: Michael about to eat lunch
jokes.punchline: The childish name for what's probably PB&J
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael childishness - the innocent way he names his sandwich

jokes.text: Didn't have to see. It was sales--I can feel it.
jokes.setup: Michael's sandwich was smashed and he's investigating
jokes.punchline: His mystical ability to sense sales department guilt
jokes.explanation: Great Michael paranoia and supernatural belief - perfectly absurd reasoning

jokes.text: The sales department smashed my sandwich. Yes. All of 'em. Together. It's a conspiracy.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining his sandwich incident
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous conspiracy theory about coordinated sandwich destruction
jokes.explanation: Excellent Michael paranoia escalation - the image of a coordinated sandwich attack is absurd

jokes.text: That's what she said. - Yeah. - Yeah.
jokes.setup: Toby saying 'You gotta do something' and 'get back on top'
jokes.punchline: Michael's automatic 'That's what she said' with Toby agreeing
jokes.explanation: Standard TWSS - gets points for being a signature joke but not particularly clever execution

jokes.text: Hand 'em over, numb nuts. But seriously, I mean, it's your job to give us those leads.
jokes.setup: Andy demanding the leads
jokes.punchline: Following 'numb nuts' with 'but seriously' and job responsibility
jokes.explanation: Good Andy tone-deafness - insulting then immediately claiming professionalism

jokes.text: Not even sabre owns this place. It's a rental.
jokes.setup: Michael saying the sales team doesn't own the place
jokes.punchline: The literal interpretation that even the company doesn't own it
jokes.explanation: Smart misdirection - Michael's literal thinking deflates his own authority play

jokes.text: I am sick of your uppity attitude...Jim.
jokes.setup: Michael listing complaints about sales staff attitude
jokes.punchline: The pause and specific callout of Jim, who's been quiet
jokes.explanation: Good targeting of Jim specifically - the pause makes it funnier

jokes.text: Just imagine that instead of going to jail for murdering someone, you got an ice cream cone. If that were the case, then in the summertime, everyone would go around killing people for the pleasure of an ice cream cone.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to explain why not to reward bad behavior
jokes.punchline: The completely insane murder-for-ice cream analogy
jokes.explanation: Legendary Michael Scott logic - completely unhinged analogy that somehow makes sense in his mind. Peak absurdist character comedy.

jokes.text: Michael, I don't want to 'incentivize' murder.
jokes.setup: Michael's murder-ice cream analogy
jokes.punchline: Gabe's completely reasonable but absurd-in-context response
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man response - treating Michael's insane analogy with corporate seriousness

jokes.text: So I am going to give the leads to...King creed. And to king...Meredith! They aren't sales people. And to king angela. Because today we are all kings. And queens.
jokes.setup: Michael announcing lead distribution after being told he must hand them out
jokes.punchline: Giving leads to non-salespeople and declaring everyone royalty
jokes.explanation: Great Michael rebellion - the absurd royal proclamation while technically following orders

jokes.text: Oh, right. Those are clues. And within each clue is a lesson. You learn a lesson, and then you find a lead.
jokes.setup: Jim questioning what he's been given instead of leads
jokes.punchline: Michael's elaborate scavenger hunt explanation
jokes.explanation: Good Michael teaching moment delusion - over-complicated solution to simple problem

jokes.text: I'm trying to make your kids respect you. Because a father needs to respect his boss, and kids don't respect the father who doesn't respect the boss. Do you understand that line of logic? I don't even think you understand it.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining his convoluted teaching method
jokes.punchline: The circular logic ending with him admitting he doesn't understand it either
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael pseudo-wisdom - convoluted reasoning that collapses on itself

jokes.text: This one's a map. Or is it?
jokes.setup: Michael showing Jim another clue
jokes.punchline: The dramatic questioning of obvious reality
jokes.explanation: Good Michael dramatic flair - treating simple things as mysterious

jokes.text: Oh, how the tables have turned.
jokes.setup: Michael seeing Andy at his desk
jokes.punchline: The cliché phrase used incorrectly in context
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael inappropriate phrase usage - sounds dramatic but doesn't fit

jokes.text: And when you're done, you can watch me shred them.
jokes.setup: Michael making Andy fill out forms for leads
jokes.punchline: The petty reveal that it's all meaningless busywork
jokes.explanation: Good Michael petty revenge - the theatrical cruelty is very in character

jokes.text: I want to watch the kardashians, okay? I don't want to watch- it's mindless! The kardashians is a good show. - Thank you, stanley! - No, you don't even know! How would he even know the kardashians?
jokes.setup: Background argument about TV shows
jokes.punchline: The escalating defense and attack of Kardashians knowledge
jokes.explanation: Decent background chaos - characters getting worked up over trivial TV preferences

jokes.text: When arrogant salesmen are mean to my face, a certain manager will go to his moppy place. He means his mopey place. It's under that street lamp that he thinks was in casablanca.
jokes.setup: Jim reading Michael's riddle about his sad place
jokes.punchline: Pam's detailed knowledge of Michael's delusions about the parking lot lamp
jokes.explanation: Great character detail - Pam's intimate knowledge of Michael's specific delusions is perfectly observed

jokes.text: God, I love you. Today I turned an office crisis into a teachable moment. A lesser manager would have screwed this day up royally.
jokes.setup: Jim expressing love to Pam, cut to Michael's talking head
jokes.punchline: Michael's self-congratulation while everything is falling apart
jokes.explanation: Good ironic juxtaposition - Michael's delusion contrasting with Jim's genuine moment

jokes.text: Yeah. I basically wish you were dead.
jokes.setup: Jim asking if Phyllis won't give him leads because he's a jerk salesman
jokes.punchline: Phyllis's matter-of-fact death wish
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Phyllis deadpan darkness - the casual delivery of extreme sentiment is perfect

jokes.text: Colder. Cold...Er. Warmer. Colder. Colder. C-- Warmer. Warmer... Hot! Hotter... Burning hot! Lower... Are you-- lower... Are you sure? Low...Er...
jokes.setup: Michael directing Jim in a hot/cold game to find leads
jokes.punchline: The increasingly specific and sexual-sounding directions
jokes.explanation: Standard hot/cold comedy with slight innuendo - functional but not exceptional

jokes.text: What are you doing, idiot? Michael's stupid scavenger hunt. Scavenger hunt? Here. I can solve it. Give it. The prankster is getting pranked. Ha ha.
jokes.setup: Dwight finding Jim doing the scavenger hunt
jokes.punchline: Dwight's gleeful 'prankster getting pranked' observation
jokes.explanation: Good Dwight satisfaction - his joy at Jim's predicament is well-earned character payoff

jokes.text: The treasure you seek is in the parking lot under the first president? Lincoln.
jokes.setup: Dwight reading a clue about the first president
jokes.punchline: His immediate wrong answer of Lincoln
jokes.explanation: Good Dwight knowledge gap - his confidence in the wrong answer is character-perfect

jokes.text: Go to the man who never breeds. kevin.
jokes.setup: Another riddle from Michael
jokes.punchline: Andy immediately identifying Kevin as someone who 'never breeds'
jokes.explanation: Brutal but funny character assassination - the immediate identification is perfectly cruel

jokes.text: You are never going to find them. - Really? - I'm going to enjoy this. Gimme the leads! Where are the leads? Gimme the leads! I'm still enjoying it!
jokes.setup: Kevin taunting Andy about not finding the leads
jokes.punchline: Kevin maintaining his enjoyment throughout Andy's increasing desperation
jokes.explanation: Good Kevin rare moment of power - his sustained enjoyment of Andy's frustration works well

jokes.text: They're in the trash. the trash. It's code. All right, meredith. Take off your dress. Okey-dokey. No. Dear god, no. It's in the trash can.
jokes.setup: Kevin saying leads are 'in the trash' and Andy thinking it's code
jokes.punchline: Andy's wrong interpretation leading to Meredith starting to undress
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection comedy - Andy's overthinking meets Meredith's eagerness for chaos

jokes.text: It's coming off either way.
jokes.setup: Being told not to take off her dress
jokes.punchline: Meredith's commitment to undressing regardless
jokes.explanation: Perfect Meredith moment - the inevitability of her inappropriate behavior is character gold

jokes.text: I emptied it in the dumpster. It had toby's baba ganoush. If we don't patronize the only syrian restaurant in town, there'll be nothing left but pan pizzas and, you know... make your own salads.
jokes.setup: Kevin explaining why he emptied the trash
jokes.punchline: His unexpectedly sophisticated concern about culinary diversity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Kevin character subversion - unexpected cultural awareness from the simple character

jokes.text: Tonight is ghost whisperer... So Friday.
jokes.setup: Someone asking what day it is
jokes.punchline: Creed using TV schedule as calendar
jokes.explanation: Perfect Creed logic - using television as his primary calendar system is beautifully absurd

jokes.text: Hi, you know those very valuable, expensive leads that you gave us earlier today? Well, because of a screw-up by a staff member, they're now in the city dump. Not your staff, michael. You.
jokes.setup: Michael rehearsing what he'll tell Sabre
jokes.punchline: Jim correcting that Michael is the staff member who screwed up
jokes.explanation: Good accountability moment - Jim's correction cuts through Michael's deflection

jokes.text: Then we'll all go out and get pizza, maybe catch a movie, late-night drink. Some more pizza, call it a night. Does it have to be pizza? Can we go for a falafel?
jokes.setup: Michael trying to make dump-digging sound fun
jokes.punchline: Toby's focus on food preferences while missing the point
jokes.explanation: Good character moments - Michael's desperate optimism meets Toby's practical concerns

jokes.text: You were complicit. Complicit. You were all 'successories.'
jokes.setup: Michael blaming others for being complicit
jokes.punchline: His mispronunciation mixing 'accessories' with 'successories'
jokes.explanation: Good Michael malapropism - the confusion between similar words is believable

jokes.text: There was a time you'd be pinching yourself to have the opportunity to look through a dump with me. Yeah, well, the acorn becomes the oak. Yeah. Well, sometimes the acorn just stays an acorn.
jokes.setup: Michael nostalgic about Dwight's former eagerness, Dwight using metaphor
jokes.punchline: Michael's devastating counter-metaphor about stunted growth
jokes.explanation: Brilliant metaphor battle - Michael's comeback is unexpectedly clever and cutting

jokes.text: If you don't believe me, look in my gutters.
jokes.setup: Michael's metaphor about acorns staying acorns
jokes.punchline: The literal proof in his home's gutters
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael literal follow-through - the image of acorns in gutters is both sad and funny

jokes.text: Don't use your cute baby to make us like you. She's wearing a onesie. stop it.
jokes.setup: Jim showing baby pictures during the office tension
jokes.punchline: Angela's resistance crumbling at the onesie detail
jokes.explanation: Great Angela moment - her stern resistance melting at baby cuteness is perfect character work

jokes.text: A lot of their work can be done from india.
jokes.setup: Andy talking about the non-sales staff
jokes.punchline: His casual suggestion of outsourcing colleagues' jobs
jokes.explanation: Good dark Andy moment - his callous business thinking about coworkers

jokes.text: You've changed, man. Oh, why, 'cause I have a shot at a 100k commission? When do you care about money? When I first met you, you were a wide-eyed innocent. Hey, there is nothing I can do about my wide-set eyes.
jokes.setup: Dwight lamenting Andy's change from innocence, using 'wide-eyed'
jokes.punchline: Andy's literal interpretation about his eye spacing
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - Andy's literal interpretation of metaphor is perfectly oblivious

jokes.text: I would be number two right now at a home depot! 'cause they promote from within! I hitched my wagon to a horse with no legs.
jokes.setup: Dwight explaining his alternative career path
jokes.punchline: The increasingly dramatic metaphors ending with the legless horse
jokes.explanation: Great Dwight resentment - the escalating metaphors and Home Depot pride are perfect

jokes.text: I should have been out at bars finding my soul mate, finding my wife, making babies. Nice babies you're making with the floozies at the bars! That's my wife you're talking about, man! Your made-up wife who doesn't exist?
jokes.setup: Dwight lamenting missed romantic opportunities
jokes.punchline: The escalation to defending his hypothetical wife's honor
jokes.explanation: Good escalating absurdity - Dwight getting offended about imaginary wife insults

jokes.text: You just made an enemy with the wrong guy, boy.
jokes.setup: Dwight's anger at Andy reaching a peak
jokes.punchline: The over-dramatic threat with awkward phrasing
jokes.explanation: Good Dwight dramatics - the awkward phrasing makes the threat more comedic than menacing

jokes.text: That's my spool!
jokes.setup: Physical fight between Dwight and Andy
jokes.punchline: Dwight's protective claim over a spool during the fight
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy detail - Dwight's possessiveness extending to random objects

jokes.text: Erin just texted me back. 'people love shells from faraway beaches.'
jokes.setup: The sales team trying to figure out peace offerings
jokes.punchline: Erin's completely unhelpful but earnest advice
jokes.explanation: Perfect Erin moment - her well-meaning but completely impractical advice is character gold

jokes.text: No other animal on earth could do this. Maybe beavers... But not like this.
jokes.setup: Michael contemplating human achievement at the dump
jokes.punchline: His consideration and dismissal of beaver capabilities
jokes.explanation: Great Michael philosophical moment - his serious consideration of beaver construction abilities

jokes.text: We get it-- you eat like royalty. No, no. This is a representation of how we feel. And how we feel is really sorry.
jokes.setup: Stanley assuming the food spread is showing off
jokes.punchline: Jim's earnest explanation of the peace offering symbolism
jokes.explanation: Good character interaction - Stanley's cynicism meeting Jim's genuine gesture

jokes.text: 2% milk! What I forgot for the coffee. Yeah, treats, stanley. They've accepted our simple offer of treats only, nothing more.
jokes.setup: Andy starting to mention their 2% commission deal
jokes.punchline: Quick cover-up about milk and Jim's emphasis on 'nothing more'
jokes.explanation: Good quick thinking comedy - the near-miss revelation and cover-up

jokes.text: Purple much? - Yeah, she does. She loves purple.
jokes.setup: Finding something purple at the dump for Phyllis
jokes.punchline: The casual confirmation of Phyllis's purple obsession
jokes.explanation: Simple character callback - acknowledging Phyllis's known color preference

jokes.text: Just this whole dump, and in the middle, one flower. - Mm-hmm. - That's it. And the caption would read... Hope... Grows... In a dump.
jokes.setup: Michael describing an artistic photo concept at the dump
jokes.punchline: His over-dramatic delivery of the hackneyed inspirational caption
jokes.explanation: Good Michael pseudo-artistic moment - the dramatic pauses make the cliché caption funnier

jokes.text: We have an awesome beanbag chair that's perfect for the break room. Eech! I'm never gonna sit on that disgusting seat. Yeah, damn right, you're not, 'cause it's for me and michael only!
jokes.setup: Finding furniture at the dump
jokes.punchline: Andy's immediate possessiveness over the gross beanbag chair
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Andy's immediate exclusivity over something nobody wants

jokes.text: You're the nicest person I've ever met.
jokes.setup: Dwight giving Michael his jacket in the cold
jokes.punchline: Michael's genuine, childlike gratitude for basic kindness
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment - Michael's genuine appreciation shows his vulnerability and need for kindness
